# Aigon

> Aigon is a CLI-first, vendor-independent workflow for spec-driven AI development and multi-agent orchestration. It manages research, feature delivery, and feedback loops across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, and Cursor.

## Getting Started

- [Getting Started](https://aigon.build/docs/getting-started): Install Aigon and run your first spec-driven feature loop.

## Concepts

- [Core Concepts](https://aigon.build/docs/concepts): The three workflows (features, research, feedback) and four execution modes that make up Aigon.
- [Execution Modes](https://aigon.build/docs/concepts/execution-modes): Drive, Fleet, Autopilot, and Swarm — four modes across two axes of agent count and autonomy.
- [Feature Evaluation](https://aigon.build/docs/concepts/evaluation): Structured comparison of Fleet implementations with rubrics and scoring.

## Guides

- [Drive Mode](https://aigon.build/docs/guides/drive-mode): Hands-on implementation with a single agent — the default way to build features.
- [Fleet Mode](https://aigon.build/docs/guides/fleet-mode): Orchestrate competing implementations across multiple agents in parallel.
- [Autopilot Mode](https://aigon.build/docs/guides/autopilot-mode): Autonomous retry loop until validation passes.
- [Dashboard](https://aigon.build/docs/guides/dashboard): Visual pipeline for monitoring features, agents, and statistics across repos.
- [Research Workflow](https://aigon.build/docs/guides/research-workflow): Investigate options with one or more agents before committing to a feature approach.
- [Feedback Workflow](https://aigon.build/docs/guides/feedback-workflow): Capture user input and route it back into your product workflow.

## Reference

- [Agents](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/agents): Supported agents, installation, and how context reaches each one.
- [Configuration](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/configuration): Configuration files, scopes, profiles, and options.
- [File Structure](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/file-structure): How Aigon organises features, research, and feedback in your repository.

### Feature Commands

- [feature-create](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-create): Create a new feature spec.
- [feature-prioritise](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-prioritise): Move a feature from inbox to backlog.
- [feature-start](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-start): Start implementation in a worktree with one or more agents.
- [feature-now](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-now): Create and immediately start a feature in one step.
- [feature-do](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-do): Send follow-up instructions to a running agent.
- [feature-open](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-open): Open a worktree terminal session for a feature.
- [feature-submit](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-submit): Mark an agent's implementation as ready for review.
- [feature-validate](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-validate): Run validation checks against a feature implementation.
- [feature-eval](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-eval): Evaluate and compare Fleet implementations.
- [feature-review](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-review): Review a submitted feature implementation.
- [feature-close](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-close): Merge the winning implementation and close the feature.
- [feature-cleanup](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-cleanup): Remove worktrees and branches for a closed feature.
- [feature-reset](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-reset): Reset a feature back to a previous state.
- [feature-autopilot](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feature/feature-autopilot): Run a feature in autonomous retry mode.

### Research Commands

- [research-create](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/research/research-create): Create a new research topic.
- [research-prioritise](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/research/research-prioritise): Move a research topic from inbox to backlog.
- [research-start](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/research/research-start): Start research with one or more agents.
- [research-open](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/research/research-open): Open a research worktree session.
- [research-do](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/research/research-do): Send follow-up instructions to a research agent.
- [research-submit](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/research/research-submit): Mark research findings as ready for evaluation.
- [research-eval](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/research/research-eval): Evaluate findings from multiple research agents.
- [research-close](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/research/research-close): Close a completed research topic.
- [research-autopilot](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/research/research-autopilot): Run research in autonomous mode.

### Feedback Commands

- [feedback-create](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feedback/feedback-create): Create a new feedback item.
- [feedback-list](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feedback/feedback-list): List feedback items.
- [feedback-triage](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/feedback/feedback-triage): Triage feedback into actionable items.

### Infrastructure Commands

- [board](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/infra/board): Display the Kanban board in the terminal.
- [dashboard](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/infra/dashboard): Launch the browser-based dashboard.
- [status](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/infra/status): Show current project status.
- [agent-status](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/infra/agent-status): Show status of running agents.
- [sessions-close](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/infra/sessions-close): Close tmux agent sessions.
- [conductor](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/infra/conductor): Manage the conductor process.
- [dev-server](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/infra/dev-server): Manage development servers for worktrees.
- [deploy](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/infra/deploy): Deploy the project.
- [insights](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/infra/insights): View workflow insights and analytics.
- [next](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/infra/next): Show suggested next action.
- [help](https://aigon.build/docs/reference/commands/infra/help): Show help information.

## Comparisons

- [Comparisons](https://aigon.build/docs/comparisons): How Aigon compares to Cursor, Copilot Workspace, Kiro, and other AI development tools.
